2605.28338.title: SafeMed-R1: Clinician-Audited Safety and Ethics Alignment for Medical Large Language Models
2605.28338.paper_summary: Large language models(LLMs) increasingly match expert performance on licensing examinations, yet routine clinical use remains limited because governance requires auditable reasoning, safety and ethics alignment, and resilience to adversarial misuse. Here we present SafeMed-R1, trained with a traceable Clinical Trust Signals(CTS) pipeline that links each reasoning instance to clinician rubric scores and edit histories, and aligned through safety and ethics supervision and red team stress testing. SafeMed-R1 attains a macro-averaged accuracy of 79.6% across clinical benchmarks. Under adversarial safety testing, it shows the lowest aggregated risk and reduces unsafe outputs by about 3 to 5% relative to its baseline. In a paired expert study of 30 medication safety vignettes, SafeMed-R1 matches PGY1 and PGY2 residents on medical correctness and scores higher for medication safety, guideline consistency, and clinical usefulness. Collectively, these results suggest that clinician-audited supervision provenance, together with domain-tailored safety and ethics alignment, can strengthen governance-relevant evidence without relying on inference-time retrieval or citation grounding.
2605.28338.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）在執業考試中的表現越來越接近專家水準，但由於治理需要可審計的推理、安全和倫理對齊，以及對抗性濫用的韌性，常規臨床使用仍然有限。在此，我們介紹SafeMed-R1，這是一個經過可追溯的臨床信任信號（CTS）管道訓練的模型，該管道將每個推理實例與臨床醫生的評分標準和編輯歷史連結，並通過安全和倫理監督及紅隊壓力測試進行對齊。SafeMed-R1在臨床基準上達到了79.6%的宏觀平均準確率。在對抗性安全測試中，它顯示出最低的綜合風險，並相對於基線減少了約3%到5%的不安全輸出。在一項針對30個藥物安全小品的配對專家研究中，SafeMed-R1在醫療正確性上與PGY1和PGY2住院醫師相匹配，並在藥物安全、指導方針一致性和臨床實用性方面得分更高。總體而言，這些結果表明，臨床醫生審核的監督來源，加上針對領域的安全和倫理對齊，可以在不依賴推理時檢索或引用基礎的情況下，加強與治理相關的證據。
2605.28338.author: Chao Ding et.al.
2605.28338.authors: Chao Ding, Mouxiao Bian, Tianbin Li, Minjia Yuan, Yidong Jiang, Yankai Jiang, Jinru Ding, Jiayuan Chen, Zhuangzhi Gao, Pengcheng Chen, Zhao He, Rongzhao Zhang, Meiling Liu, Luyi Jiang, Jie Xu
